title: Reading and Use of English – Part 4
description: For questions 25–30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between THREE and SIX words, including the word given.

questions.firstSentence: The concert was so popular that tickets sold out immediately.
questions.afterGap: the concert that tickets sold out immediately.
questions.explanation: Tests 'such + adj + noun' structure expressing high degree.

questions.firstSentence: People believe that the painting was stolen by a professional thief.
questions.explanation: Tests passive voice with reporting verbs.

questions.firstSentence: You should have called me when you arrived at the station.
questions.afterGap: me when you arrived at the station.
questions.explanation: Tests obligation in the past using 'supposed to'.

questions.firstSentence: I regret not taking my doctor's advice.
questions.explanation: Tests expressing regret using 'wish + past perfect'.

questions.firstSentence: Lucy didn't pass the exam because she didn't study hard.
questions.beforeGap: Lucy could have passed the exam
questions.explanation: Tests third conditionals expressing past hypothetical situations.

questions.firstSentence: Never before have I seen such fantastic fireworks.
questions.explanation: Tests inversion in negative adverbial phrases.
